question: Define normalization in database management systems and state its primary goal.
List the ACID properties of a database transaction and briefly describe each.
State the purpose of a primary key in a relational database schema.
Identify the four standard isolation levels defined by the SQL standard for transactions.
Name the three types of anomalies that arise from an un-normalised relational table.
Define the OSI model and state the function of each of its seven layers.
Identify the fields contained in an IPv4 packet header.
State the difference between TCP and UDP in terms of connection establishment.
List the conditions that must hold simultaneously for deadlock to occur in an operating system.
Define a page fault and state under what condition it is triggered.
Identify the scheduling criterion optimised by the Shortest Job First algorithm.
State the four phases of the Unified Software Development Life Cycle in Agile development.
Define cohesion and coupling in the context of software module design.
List the five SOLID principles used in object-oriented software design.
Define a binary search tree and state its ordering property.
Identify the time complexity of searching in a balanced AVL tree.
State the difference between a stack and a queue in terms of element access order.
Explain how a B-tree index improves the performance of SQL SELECT queries in a database.
Describe the two-phase commit protocol and explain why it guarantees atomicity across distributed databases.
Explain the difference between a clustered index and a non-clustered index in a relational database.
Describe how the three-way handshake establishes a TCP connection between a client and a server.
Explain how DHCP automatically assigns IP addresses to devices on a network.
Describe the role of ARP in resolving IP addresses to MAC addresses on a local area network.
Explain how the banker's algorithm prevents deadlock in a multi-process operating system.
Describe the difference between preemptive and non-preemptive scheduling in operating systems.
Explain how version control systems such as Git support collaborative software development.
Describe the role of a design pattern and explain how the Singleton pattern enforces a single instance.
Explain how depth-first search traverses a graph and identify its time complexity.
Describe the collision resolution strategy used by open addressing in a hash table.
Explain how a min-heap maintains its structural and ordering properties after insertion.
Explain how gradient descent minimises the loss function during neural network training.
Describe the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning and give one application of each.
Explain how a convolutional neural network extracts spatial features from an input image.
Describe the concept of the A* search algorithm and explain why it is considered optimal.
Given a relation in first normal form with transitive dependencies, apply the rules of third normal form to decompose it.
Apply the concept of indexing to design an efficient query plan for a table with 10 million rows.
Given a network topology, apply Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path between two routers.
Apply CIDR notation to calculate the number of usable hosts in a subnet with a /26 prefix.
Demonstrate how to implement the producer-consumer problem using semaphores in an operating system context.
Apply the Round Robin scheduling algorithm to compute average waiting time for a given set of processes.
Use UML class diagrams to model a library management system with members, books, and loans.
Apply Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm to a weighted directed graph and trace its execution step by step.
Implement a merge sort algorithm and trace its execution on the array [5, 2, 8, 1, 9, 3].
Apply the k-nearest neighbours algorithm to classify a new data point in a two-dimensional feature space.
Use the naive Bayes classifier to compute the probability that a given email is spam based on word frequencies.
Apply the minimax algorithm with alpha-beta pruning to determine the optimal move in a given game tree.
Apply RSA encryption to encode a short plaintext message using given public key parameters.
Demonstrate how a SQL injection attack exploits an unsanitised login form and how parameterised queries prevent it.
Given a set of processes and a resource allocation table, apply the resource allocation graph algorithm to detect deadlock.
Apply the principles of structured query language to write a stored procedure that calculates cumulative sales by quarter.
Analyse the trade-offs between using a relational database and a document-oriented NoSQL database for a high-volume social media platform.
Break down the components of the query execution engine and explain how each stage contributes to query optimisation.
Compare and contrast OSPF and BGP routing protocols in terms of scope, convergence speed, and use case.
Examine the causes of TCP congestion and analyse how the slow start and congestion avoidance phases respond.
Determine the root cause of priority inversion in a real-time operating system and analyse its impact on system correctness.
Compare the memory management strategies of paging and segmentation and analyse the fragmentation each produces.
Analyse the impact of high coupling between software modules on maintainability and testability of a large codebase.
Examine the architectural flaws in a provided monolithic application and analyse the effort required to migrate it to microservices.
Compare the average-case and worst-case time complexities of quicksort and heapsort and analyse when each is preferred.
Analyse why a hash table with a poor hash function degrades to O(n) search performance and propose a remedy.
Compare bias and variance as sources of model error and analyse the effect of increasing model complexity on each.
Examine the differences between batch gradient descent, stochastic gradient descent, and mini-batch gradient descent for large datasets.
Analyse the architectural differences between a rule-based expert system and a neural network classifier for medical diagnosis.
Examine how a man-in-the-middle attack exploits weaknesses in certificate validation during a TLS handshake.
Analyse the security implications of storing session tokens in browser cookies versus local storage in a web application.
Compare the performance characteristics of linked lists and dynamic arrays for insertions, deletions, and random access.
Evaluate the suitability of eventual consistency versus strong consistency for a globally distributed e-commerce inventory system.
Assess whether de-normalisation is an appropriate optimisation strategy for a read-heavy reporting database and justify your decision.
Critique the use of a flat network topology for a university campus with five thousand devices and recommend an alternative.
Evaluate the trade-offs of using a software-defined network versus a traditional hardware-based network in a data centre.
Justify whether a ticketing system or a token-based mechanism is more suitable for mutual exclusion in a distributed operating system.
Assess the correctness and efficiency of the LRU page replacement algorithm relative to the optimal algorithm.
Evaluate the decision to adopt test-driven development in a safety-critical embedded software project and defend your recommendation.
Critique a provided software requirements specification document and identify ambiguities that could lead to implementation defects.
Assess whether a red-black tree or a skip list is a more appropriate data structure for an ordered key-value store.
Evaluate the trade-offs between using an adjacency matrix and an adjacency list for representing sparse graphs.
Justify the choice of a random forest model over a single decision tree for a fraud detection system.
Evaluate whether a recurrent neural network or a transformer architecture is more appropriate for long-document summarisation.
Assess the ethical implications of deploying a facial recognition system in public spaces and recommend governance policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-factor authentication compared to single-factor authentication for securing enterprise systems.
Critique the zero-trust security model and assess its feasibility for a small business with limited IT resources.
Assess whether agile or waterfall methodology is more appropriate for developing a safety-critical avionics control system.
Evaluate the impact of using a convolutional neural network versus a vision transformer for real-time object detection on edge devices.
Design a relational schema for a hospital management system that records patients, doctors, appointments, and prescriptions in third normal form.
Propose a sharding strategy for a social network database with two billion user accounts to achieve horizontal scalability.
Design a reliable file transfer protocol over UDP that guarantees ordered delivery and retransmission of lost packets.
Construct a network architecture for a bank that separates internet-facing services from internal systems using DMZ and firewall zones.
Design a custom memory allocator for an embedded operating system with no virtual memory support and fixed-size partitions.
Formulate a CPU scheduling algorithm that minimises average turnaround time for a mixed workload of I/O-bound and CPU-bound processes.
Design a microservices architecture for an online retail platform including service boundaries, communication patterns, and data ownership.
Develop a comprehensive CI/CD pipeline for a Python web application including build, test, security scan, and deployment stages.
Construct a self-balancing trie data structure to support autocomplete search over a dictionary of one million words.
Design an LRU cache using a combination of a doubly linked list and a hash map and describe its get and put operations.
Propose a novel feature engineering pipeline for a customer churn prediction model trained on transactional banking data.
Design a reinforcement learning environment and reward function to train an agent to optimise warehouse robot routing.
Develop a knowledge representation scheme using semantic networks to model medical diagnostic reasoning for a clinical decision support system.
Design a multi-agent system for coordinating autonomous vehicles at a busy intersection without centralised control.
Design an intrusion detection system that combines signature-based and anomaly-based detection for a corporate network.
Propose a key management architecture for encrypting sensitive data at rest in a multi-tenant cloud environment.
Design a scalable event-driven architecture for a real-time ride-hailing platform supporting millions of concurrent users.
